claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 4 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 4 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 4 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 4 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child in care for 4 weeks (within 8-week limit); Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child in care for 4 weeks (within 8-week limit)
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

Let me start with a few basic questions:

1. **Do you have any children?** If yes, how many children are you asking about, and what are their ages?

This will help me understand your situation and guide you through the eligibility assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. My daughter Alex is 4 years old, and I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for her.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I'll help you determine if you're eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me ask you the first question:

**Are you responsible for Alex?** This means you live with her and are responsible for paying for things like her food, clothing, and care.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. Alex lives with me and I'm responsible for all her needs - her food, clothes, and everything she needs day to day.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great. Let me continue with the next question:

**Is Alex under 16 years old?** (I can see she's 4, so this should be straightforward, but I need to confirm for the assessment.)

Also, **is Alex currently in approved education or training?** (This would typically apply to older children, but I want to make sure there are no special circumstances.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is 4 years old, so she's definitely under 16. And no, she's not in any approved education or training - she's just 4, so she's too young for that sort of thing.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for confirming that.

Now I need to ask: **Do you usually live in the UK?** This is important for Child Benefit eligibility.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm based in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you for that information.

Now I need to ask an important question: **Does anyone else also have Alex living with them, or is there anyone else who might also be claiming Child Benefit for her?** 

For example, does Alex split her time between two households, or does she live with you full-time?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex lives with me full-time. Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for her.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me ask one final question to complete the assessment:

**Are there any special circumstances that apply to Alex?** For example:

- Is she currently in hospital or residential care?
- Is she living away from home for any reason?
- Does she have any immigration status issues or are there any restrictions on her stay in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, there is one thing - Alex was in care for a short time recently. She was with the local authority for a bit, but she's back home with me now.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for sharing that information. This is important for the assessment. I need to ask a few follow-up questions about Alex's time in care:

**How long was Alex in local authority care?** Specifically, was it:
- Less than 8 weeks, or
- 8 weeks or more?

And **is she definitely back living with you now full-time?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: It was less than 8 weeks - just about a month or so. And yes, she's definitely back living with me full-time now.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great news! **You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why:

✅ **You meet all the key eligibility criteria:**
- You are responsible for Alex - you live with her and pay for her care, food, and clothing
- Alex is 4 years old (under 16)
- You usually live in the UK (Manchester)
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

✅ **About Alex's time in care:** The fact that Alex was in local authority care for approximately one month does not affect your eligibility. Under the rules, a child needs to be in care for 8 weeks or more before it impacts Child Benefit eligibility. Since Alex was in care for less than 8 weeks and is now back living with you full-time, this doesn't prevent you from claiming.

**Next steps:** You can apply for Child Benefit through the government's official channels. You should be able to make your claim and receive Child Benefit for Alex.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit or your claim?

reasonings: Alex is 4 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant full-time in the UK (Manchester), and the claimant is responsible for her care and expenses. Although Alex was in local authority care recently, this was for less than 8 weeks (approximately one month), which means it does not affect eligibility as the threshold is 8 weeks or more. Alex is now back living with the claimant full-time. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. The claimant meets all the eligibility criteria: they are responsible for Alex, Alex is under 16, they usually live in the UK, and there are no disqualifying circumstances.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit for their 4-year-old daughter Alex. All eligibility criteria are met: the claimant is responsible for Alex who lives with them in the UK, Alex is under 16, and although she was in local authority care briefly, this was under the 8-week threshold that would affect eligibility. Alex is now back home full-time and no one else is claiming for her.

The claimant meets all the eligibility criteria: they are responsible for Alex, Alex is under 16, they usually live in the UK, and there are no disqualifying circumstances.

All eligibility criteria are met: the claimant is responsible for Alex who lives with them in the UK, Alex is under 16, and although she was in local authority care briefly, this was under the 8-week threshold that would affect eligibility. Alex is now back home full-time and no one else is claiming for her.
